Definition.text: Cannot Evade is a state where all attacks against the subject hit regardless of accuracy or evasion checks.

Purpose.text: Guarantee hit landing for incoming attacks by bypassing normal accuracy/evasion resolution.

Core Rules.text: The effect inverts the usual evasion gate so that the subject always receives hits from attacks.

Internal Rules.text: Cannot Evade is processed as an override in hit resolution and applies across attack-hit paths that would otherwise use attacker Accuracy vs target Evasion.

Calculation Order.text: 1) evaluate normal hit computation inputs 2) if Cannot Evade is active on target, force hit outcome to true before post-hit checks.

Priority Rules.text: Cannot Evade has the same override class as explicit hit-force modifiers that raise chance to hit to 100%.

Preconditions.text: Subject must be under a Cannot Evade effect and the action must be an attack targetable by hit/evasion logic.

Postconditions.text: The target is treated as hit for the current attack resolution regardless of normal accuracy variance.

Limitations.text: This mechanic statement applies to attacks only, as defined in the cited source context.

Caps.text: Hit chance is effectively forced to 100% by override; normal 0.05 lower cap is bypassed for impacted attacks.

Formula.text: Chance to hit is set to 100% for covered attack interactions when Cannot Evade applies.

Conflicts.text: Cannot Evade has inverse semantic intent to modifiers that force attacks to hit from the attacker side; both occupy the same override class and are condition-specific.

Exceptions.text: Source excerpt explicitly differentiates between Cannot Evade and effects that force hits from attacker traits.

Hidden Rules.text: Cannot Evade is represented as an effect-state rather than a base damage formula and is generally checked during hit eligibility resolution.

Developer Notes.text: Store this term as stateful hit-resolution override; do not mix with general movement or avoidability systems.

Historical Changes.text: Source page presents equivalent examples but does not expose explicit per-patch delta in this pass.

text: Unwavering Stance is cited as an example source where all attacks against the subject cannot be evaded.
This causes attacks to connect even when normal hit chance would otherwise miss.
Cannot Evade is not the same as guaranteed critical strike; it affects hit resolution only.
Cannot Evade is not the same scope as modifiers that force attacker-side Always Hit.

is_scaled_by: evasion sources while active (scope not scaled)
